steps.title: Focus on programmed rules
steps.content: The underlined claim says grid-cell-like behavior in neural networks comes less from real similarity to biological brains and more from rules programmed into the networks. A supporting finding must therefore show that the programmed rules, rather than brain-like function, are what produce the apparent grid-cell behavior.

steps.title: Support the source of behavior
steps.content: A does exactly that: if the rules allowing grid-cell-like behavior have no equivalent in biological brains, then the similarity is only an artifact of programming. B concerns networks that do not show the behavior. C shifts to multiple cell types instead of grid cells. D describes training after programming but does not explain where the grid-cell-like behavior comes from. The answer is A.
